parameters.content:
### 1. `users` Table
Stores the core user credentials and links the user to a specific role.

| Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **id** | Integer / UUID | Primary Key | Unique identifier for the user. |
| **username** | Varchar | Unique, Not Null | The user's login name. |
| **password_hash** | Varchar | Not Null | Securely hashed password. |
| **role_id** | Integer / UUID | Foreign Key | Links to `roles.id`. |
| **created_at** | Timestamp | Default: Current | When the account was created. |

---

### 2. `roles` Table
Defines the high-level roles available in your system.

| Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **id** | Integer / UUID | Primary Key | Unique identifier for the role. |
| **name** | Varchar | Unique, Not Null | The name of the role (e.g., 'admin'). |
| **description** | Text | Nullable | Optional details about the role's purpose. |

---

### 3. `role_permissions` Table
Maps specific granular permissions to a role.

| Column Name | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **id** | Integer / UUID | Primary Key | Unique identifier for the mapping. |
| **role_id** | Integer / UUID | Foreign Key | Links to `roles.id`. |
| **permission** | Varchar | Not Null | The specific action allowed (e.g., 'read:reports'). |

---

parameters.query:
-- =============================================
-- Universo Tintas — 001: User CRUD Functions
-- Operates directly on auth.users (no profiles)
-- =============================================

-- =======  UP  ========

DROP FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users();

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users()
RETURNS TABLE(
  user_id    UUID,
  email      TEXT,
  full_name  TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
AS $$
  SELECT
    id AS user_id,
    email::TEXT,
    COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', '')::TEXT AS full_name,
    created_at
  FROM auth.users
  ORDER BY created_at DESC;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_confirm_user(p_user_id UUID)
RETURNS VOID
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  UPDATE auth.users
  SET email_confirmed_at = NOW(),
      updated_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(
  p_user_id   UUID,
  p_full_name TEXT
)
RETURNS VOID
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  UPDATE auth.users
  SET raw_user_meta_data = COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data, '{}'::jsonb)
                           || jsonb_build_object('full_name', p_full_name),
      updated_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_delete_user(p_user_id UUID)
RETURNS VOID
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  DELETE FROM auth.users WHERE id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_confirm_user(UUID) TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(UUID, TEXT) TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_delete_user(UUID) TO authenticated;

NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';

-- =============================================
-- Universo Tintas — 002: Add role to user CRUD
-- Run AFTER 001_user_crud_functions.sql
-- =============================================

-- =======  UP  ========

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS universo_t_admin_list_users();
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users()
RETURNS TABLE(
  user_id    UUID,
  email      TEXT,
  full_name  TEXT,
  role       TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
AS $$
  SELECT
    id AS user_id,
    email::TEXT,
    COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', '')::TEXT AS full_name,
    COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data->>'role', 'visualizador')::TEXT AS role,
    created_at
  FROM auth.users
  ORDER BY created_at DESC;
$$;

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS universo_t_admin_update_user(UUID, TEXT);
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(
  p_user_id   UUID,
  p_full_name TEXT,
  p_role      TEXT DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS VOID
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
DECLARE
  new_meta JSONB;
BEGIN
  new_meta := jsonb_build_object('full_name', p_full_name);
  IF p_role IS NOT NULL THEN
    new_meta := new_meta || jsonb_build_object('role', p_role);
  END IF;
  UPDATE auth.users
  SET raw_user_meta_data = COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data, '{}'::jsonb) || new_meta,
      updated_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(UUID, TEXT, TEXT) TO authenticated;

NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';

-- =======  DOWN  ========
Reverts to 001 signatures (no role)

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS universo_t_admin_list_users();
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users()
RETURNS TABLE(
  user_id    UUID,
  email      TEXT,
  full_name  TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
AS $$
  SELECT
    id AS user_id,
    email::TEXT,
    COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', '')::TEXT AS full_name,
    created_at
  FROM auth.users
  ORDER BY created_at DESC;
$$;

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS universo_t_admin_update_user(UUID, TEXT, TEXT);
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(
  p_user_id   UUID,
  p_full_name TEXT
)
RETURNS VOID
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = auth, public
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  UPDATE auth.users
  SET raw_user_meta_data = COALESCE(raw_user_meta_data, '{}'::jsonb)
                           || jsonb_build_object('full_name', p_full_name),
      updated_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = p_user_id;
END;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_list_users() TO authenticated;
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION universo_t_admin_update_user(UUID, TEXT) TO authenticated;

NOTIFY pgrst, 'reload schema';
